pGeneralRules: Most 2-syllable verbs are stressed on the second syllable, while most 2-syllable nouns and adjectives are stressed on the first syllable.
h4FrenchLatin: French / Latin Origin → Stress on 2nd syllable
pPractice1: I would like to argue that this solution is better.
pPractice2: We should focus on the main problem.
pPractice4: The policy does not offer enough support.
pPractice5: I will present three main ideas.
pRuleFirstStress: Most are stressed on the first syllable.
pWarningSecondStress: BUT adjectives of French origin or starting with 'a-' are often stressed on the second syllable.
pCommentNote: COMMENT has the same stress in noun and verb.
pEndingsRule: Words ending in -tion / -sion, -ic and -ity are stressed on the syllable before.
